title: eupatorium hirsutum var. triseriale
intro: Eupatorium hirsutum var. triseriale, also known as American feverfew, is a variety of the Eupatorium hirsutum plant, a perennial herb native to North America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones and other bioactive compounds. The plant is believed to provide health benefits such as reducing fever, alleviating pain and inflammation, and supporting the immune system. Eupatorium hirsutum var. triseriale is typically used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its effects.
